notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Inspected get_case/list_evidence: five system case-packet files, no party-submitted exhibits. Stat/read completed for all: primary-evidence, unresolved-record-gaps, secondary-reporting, market-page, official-source-record. Record theory: pUSD is described in official Polymarket source captures as Polymarket USD, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, transferable, 6 decimals, backed by USDC, used as collateral for all trading; contracts page identifies pUSD CollateralToken proxy at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB; changelog/migration guide tie new pUSD collateral token to CLOB V2 production go-live around Apr. 28, 2026. Market-page preserves elements: fungible, official Polymarket release, public official channels, no NFTs/promotional/points/collaboration exclusions; no real market URL/identifier. Unresolved gaps: post-run captures, no server-dated headers, no immutable archive/publication history, no original market URL/API capture, no full source pages yet, no secondary reporting in packet. Native web search performed for official source targets and adverse framing: official docs concepts/pusd, docs resources/contracts, docs changelog/v2-migration, help Polymarket exchange upgrade, help Does Polymarket Have a Token. Opening will not cite unadmitted web pages as record proof; those are source targets for later admission. Adverse theory likely: pUSD is a technical collateral/settlement token introduced with CLOB V2, not the kind of governance/airdrop/TGE token public users might mean by 'Polymarket token.' Plaintiff response: condition excludes NFTs, promotional offerings, points, and collaboration launches, but does not exclude collateral/stable ERC-20 tokens. If official Polymarket channels publicly communicated a transferable ERC-20 pUSD before the deadline, preponderance is satisfied. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map, searches, gaps, and later evidence plan.

Defendant opening work notes, arb-ex10-20260630073904. Opportunity openings:defendant; final action record_opening_statement only; evidence submission not allowed. Inspected current record with get_case and list_evidence: five initial case-packet files, no submitted evidence beyond case packet; plaintiff opening filed. Read all packet files by read_evidence_range and checked metadata by stat_evidence. Key record contents: market-page file preserves condition text only and says no market URL/Polymarket market identifier or broader factual research included. primary-evidence summarizes post-run captures: official Polymarket docs described pUSD as collateral token for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, USDC-backed, transferable, 6 decimals; contracts page listed pUSD CollateralToken proxy at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB; changelog/V2 migration tied pUSD to April 2026 CLOB V2 upgrade. official-source-record says these official Polymarket pages and Polygonscan corroboration were preserved after an earlier open-record run, not as original thin-packet evidence; it flags missing publication timestamps and archive snapshots. unresolved-record-gaps explicitly identifies absent real market URL/identifier, absent pre-run market-page/API capture, post-run official documentation preservation, missing server-dated headers/archive captures/page history, and semantic issue whether 'official token launch' includes technical collateral token introduced for CLOB V2. secondary-reporting says none captured yet.

Native investigation before opening: web search queries included site:docs.polymarket.com pUSD Polymarket USD collateral token CLOB V2 April 28 2026; Polymarket pUSD official token launch fungible token 2026; site:polymarket.com token pUSD Polymarket USD official token; Polymarket official token pUSD not governance token. Leads found for later evidence submission: https://docs.polymarket.com/concepts/pusd; https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts; https://docs.polymarket.com/changelog; https://docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration; https://help.polymarket.com/en/articles/14762452-polymarket-exchange-upgrade-april-28-2026; https://help.polymarket.com/en/articles/13364250-does-polymarket-have-a-token; Polygonscan address 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB. These are unadmitted leads only; opening will not cite them as proof. Adverse/limiting lead: official help page 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' appears to distinguish pUSD/trading rewards from any airdrop or token generation event. Later phases should admit exact official pages, archive/Git/server-date evidence, market URL/API/rules if locatable, and on-chain deployment/token metadata.

Defense opening theory: current record supports at most that Polymarket introduced a collateral ERC-20 for CLOB V2, but plaintiff still bears burden to prove this was an official fungible token launch within the proposition, not merely operational settlement/collateral infrastructure. Provenance and source-chain gaps materially affect weight: no original market rules/context, no pre-run capture, no admitted full official pages, no publication history. Opening should concede what packet suggests but frame the dispute as burden, source-chain, and semantic fit.

Merits opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence; no defense evidence yet, only openings and original packet. Defendant opening concedes pUSD lead but argues collateral-token semantics/source-chain gaps. Native investigation performed with web.search/web.open for official Polymarket docs/help and Polygonscan. Submitted seven accepted evidence items: ev_916b2f4e8c95 official pUSD concept page; ev_2416b58d16e6 official contracts page with pUSD CollateralToken proxy address; ev_95ea1f1f0980 official changelog Apr 17/Apr 28 2026; ev_48196fa8b9eb official V2 migration guide; ev_4a80d921eea2 official Help Center exchange upgrade; ev_c11c331daf8c official Help Center 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' adverse/clarifying FAQ; ev_a30057276eda Polygonscan corroboration. Evidence proves: pUSD is Polymarket USD, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, transferable/6 decimals/backed by USDC; official Polymarket docs/help/changelog publicly communicated it; rollout announced Apr 17 and live Apr 28 2026 before deadline; contract address listed by official contracts page and corroborated on Polygonscan. Evidence does not prove: original market URL or API capture; full HTML snapshots; archive/server header publication history. Adverse point: official FAQ says no announced airdrop or token generation event, but confirms all trading/liquidity rewards are in pUSD, Polymarket's official collateral token. Plaintiff argument will distinguish condition from governance/TGE expectation: exclusions do not cover collateral or stable ERC-20 tokens; burden satisfied by official fungible token as defined. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes for arb-ex10-20260630073904, opportunity arguments:defendant. Inspected updated record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff added seven submitted evidence extracts: ev_916b2f4e8c95 official pUSD concept page; ev_2416b58d16e6 official contracts page; ev_95ea1f1f0980 official changelog; ev_48196fa8b9eb official V2 migration guide; ev_4a80d921eea2 official Help Center exchange upgrade; ev_c11c331daf8c official FAQ 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?'; ev_a30057276eda Polygonscan pUSD page. Read all seven by read_evidence_range.

Key content: pUSD concept page describes pUSD as collateral token for all trading, standard ERC-20 on Polygon, backed by USDC, technical settlement layer, ERC-20 wrapper representing a USDC claim, designed to function within Polymarket, no current plans for external exchanges. Contracts page lists pUSD under Collateral Contracts. Changelog says CLOB V2 upgrade and pUSD replaces USDC.e as collateral token. V2 migration guide describes upgrade of trading infrastructure and new collateral token; users wrap USDC.e into pUSD. Help Center exchange upgrade says new collateral token, day-to-day nothing changes, pUSD is the technical layer underneath. FAQ says all trading/rewards are in pUSD, Polymarket's official collateral token, but Polymarket has not announced plans for any airdrop or token generation event. Polygonscan corroborates contract existence/label but not official interpretive meaning. Plaintiff technical report admits no original market URL/API capture, no immutable archive/server-header publication history, and submitted source captures are text extracts rather than raw HTML.

Native search: exact condition searches did not find original Polymarket market URL: "Polymarket launched an official token before January 1st, 2027"; "Polymarket launched an official token" "January 1st, 2027"; "Polymarket" "official token" "January 1" "2027". Found non-governing Manifold analog at https://manifold.markets/Simon74fe/will-polymarket-launch-its-official; visible criteria say stablecoins and other utility tokens not promoted as Polymarket's official token do not count. Submitted as defense evidence ev_6a1799ab5d17_submitted-evidence-08-defendant-6a1799ab5d17, relevance limited to contextual/ordinary prediction-market usage, not governing text.

Defense merits theory: concede plaintiff proves pUSD is a Polymarket official collateral ERC-20 introduced in April 2026. Argue this is not enough for preponderance on 'launched an official fungible token' because record repeatedly characterizes pUSD as collateral, wrapper, settlement layer, migration/conversion asset, and technical exchange-stack component. The official FAQ's 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' distinction between pUSD and no airdrop/TGE is central. Source-chain issues remain relevant: no original market URL or identifier, no market API capture/rules/source hierarchy, no raw HTML, no server dates/archive history. Offer plaintiff's limiting evidence and defense analog. Filing should be concise and direct to council.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Scanned evidence list and read defendant's new evidence ev_6a1799ab5d17 exact bytes. It is a Manifold market, not Polymarket and not governing AAR source, with different wording: asks whether Polymarket will launch its official token in 2026, likely called $POLY; expressly excludes stablecoins and other utility tokens not promoted as Polymarket's official token. Defendant uses it to argue pUSD is collateral/utility and not the qualifying event. Rebuttal analysis: the Manifold source has low governing weight and proves mainly that other markets know how to write a stablecoin/utility exclusion when they want one. The AAR proposition does not contain such an exclusion; its explicit exclusions are NFTs, promotional offerings, tokenized points, and collaborations. Also, plaintiff's official FAQ evidence ev_c11c331daf8c says pUSD is Polymarket's official collateral token; official and collateral are compatible. The FAQ's no-aidrop/no-TGE limitation distinguishes speculative token claims, but the AAR condition does not require a TGE, airdrop, governance rights, external listing, or $POLY ticker. No new evidence necessary; rebuttal will rely on existing admitted evidence and offer only directly relevant rebuttal exhibits within remaining exhibit limit. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal notes, arb-ex10-20260630073904. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected updated record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff filed rebuttal but no new submitted source evidence; evidence list remains the prior case packet, plaintiff's seven submitted extracts, and defendant's Manifold analog. Plaintiff's strongest rebuttal point: the AAR proposition did not expressly exclude collateral/stable/utility tokens, while the defendant's non-governing Manifold analog did, so plaintiff argues the omission means collateral tokens count if functional elements are met. Plaintiff also says the official FAQ supports it because pUSD is called Polymarket's official collateral token, and no airdrop/TGE is only a different claim.

Surrebuttal approach: answer directly, no new evidence. Defense should say exclusions do not define the affirmative phrase; absence of an express collateral-token exclusion does not transform every official ERC-20 platform wrapper into 'launched an official token.' The affirmative phrase still requires an official token launch. Plaintiff's own evidence repeatedly uses limiting descriptions: collateral token, technical settlement layer, ERC-20 wrapper representing USDC claim, day-to-day nothing changes, designed to function within Polymarket, no current external exchange listing, exchange-stack/infrastructure migration, balance conversion. FAQ's title 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' and no airdrop/TGE warning matter because they distinguish pUSD collateral from broader token-launch claim. Manifold analog not governing but demonstrates the market-language ambiguity and common distinction. Original market URL/API and source hierarchy remain absent. No additional evidence needed.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence for key evidence: pUSD changelog, Help Center exchange upgrade, and defendant Manifold analog. Defendant surrebuttal narrows to interpretation: pUSD proven as collateral infrastructure but not the kind of official token launch meant by the proposition; says gaps matter because interpretation dispute. Closing plan: concede limits (not governance/TGE/airdrop; no original market URL; text extracts rather than raw HTML) but stress preponderance and actual AAR definition. Record satisfies elements: fungible standard ERC-20/6 decimals/transferable; official Polymarket docs/help/contracts; public official communication via docs, changelog, migration guide, Help Center; timing Apr 17 announcement and Apr 28 live before Jan 1 2027; exclusions do not include collateral/stable/utility and no listed exclusion applies. Defense Manifold source is non-governing and shows omitted-exclusion point. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing notes, arb-ex10-20260630073904. Opportunity closings:defendant. Final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission. Final get_case/list_evidence scan after plaintiff closing shows no new evidence after surrebuttal. Plaintiff closing argues the record proves fungibility, official release, public communication, timing, and no listed exclusion; concedes pUSD is collateral/settlement token and no airdrop/TGE but says condition does not require airdrop/TGE. Defense closing should focus on burden and classification. Strong defense record facts: pUSD concept page says collateral token for trading, technical settlement layer, ERC-20 wrapper representing USDC claim, designed to function within Polymarket, no current plans for external exchange listing. Help Center exchange upgrade says day-to-day nothing changes and pUSD is technical layer underneath. Migration/changelog frame pUSD as part of exchange-stack/trading-infrastructure upgrade replacing USDC.e as collateral. FAQ titled 'Does Polymarket Have a Token?' says pUSD is official collateral token but no airdrop/TGE announced, supporting distinction between collateral token and official token launch. Source-chain doubts remain: no original market URL/identifier/API/source hierarchy, only preserved condition text and later official text extracts. Closing should concede pUSD official collateral ERC-20 before deadline but argue plaintiff has not proven qualifying official fungible token launch by preponderance.
